findings.evidence: File opens with `namespace TauCeti` / `open InnerProductSpace`; `HarmonicOnNhd` is `InnerProductSpace.HarmonicOnNhd` (Harmonic/Basic.lean:46). Generalized field notation looks up `InnerProductSpace.HarmonicOnNhd.comp_*`, not the declared `TauCeti.HarmonicOnNhd.comp_*`.
findings.fix: Drop these two lemmas (they are trivial `.2`-applications of the existing `_iff` lemmas and add nothing), or rename them to non-projection names; they cannot be made dot-callable without living in `InnerProductSpace.HarmonicOnNhd`.
findings.issue: `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_linearIsometryEquiv` and `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_add_right` are declared under `namespace TauCeti`, producing `TauCeti.HarmonicOnNhd.comp_*`. Dot notation `hf.comp_add_right a` on `hf : HarmonicOnNhd ...` resolves against the type's head namespace `InnerProductSpace.HarmonicOnNhd`, which has no such lemma, so the dot-notation naming is misleading and unusable as projection notation (unlike Mathlib's `HarmonicAt.comp_CLM`).

findings.evidence: Diff defines `harmonicOnNhd_comp_affineIsometryEquiv_iff` but no `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_affineIsometryEquiv`; provides `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_*` but no `HarmonicAt.comp_*`, contrasting Mathlib's symmetric `HarmonicAt.comp_CLM` + `HarmonicOnNhd.comp_CLM`.
findings.fix: Either complete the parallel set of forward forms (including the affine general case and the `HarmonicAt` level, mirroring `HarmonicAt.comp_CLM`/`HarmonicOnNhd.comp_CLM`) or remove the forward forms entirely and rely on the `*_iff` lemmas, which already cover every case.
findings.issue: The forward (non-iff) dot-notation API is asymmetric: forward forms exist only for `linearIsometryEquiv` and `add_right` on `HarmonicOnNhd`, while the general `affineIsometryEquiv` case and all `HarmonicAt.comp_*` forward forms are omitted.

summary: Surface is otherwise minimal and helpers are private, but the two HarmonicOnNhd.comp_* forward lemmas are declared inside `namespace TauCeti`, so they are named `TauCeti.HarmonicOnNhd.comp_*` and cannot be reached by dot notation on a `HarmonicOnNhd` hypothesis; the forward API is also asymmetric.
